title: SOULCALIBUR VI

cpu: AMD Ryzen 5 2400G with Radeon Vega Graphics
notes:
Title screen does not work but everything else I have played works fine, including online.

I've seen some people mention stuttering.  I believe this stuttering they are mentioning is caused by first time loading of audio.  The first time the game loads any audio (such as when you hit somebody and they go "UGH" for the first time) the game hangs slightly.  This can get ridiculous if you do something like a critical edge, which has many sound effects attached to it.  However, that is only the first time.  The second time you do that move it won't stutter, and oddly enough this carries over to multiple play-sessions.  Shutting off the game or restarting PC does not cause the move to go back to stuttering, the first time your PC ever plays that sound, it never stutters again on that move, so just play the game for half an hour and stuttering issues all but vanish.

title: SOULCALIBUR VI

notes:
Seems like it runs okay, but no matter what I do, the "up" input is held down, preventing me from playing it entirely. I tried using a variety of input devices (just my keyboard with no bluetooth drivers for my controller, keyboard w/drivers for controller, controller via bluetooth, controller directly wired).

If this one issue can be resolved, the game looks like it'd be pretty playable from there, but this one issue completely cripples it otherwise.

title: SOULCALIBUR VI

notes:
Videos are still not supported. This means the title screen background as well as Story Mode cutscenes are not working.

If you don't care about those things and have a setup similar to mine, the game should work fairly well. I recommend using Mesa ACO drivers for improved performance.

Also, I did the tweak mentioned here to make the game look even better (hate the post-processing on this game, honestly), and it worked perfectly well, too! https://pcgamingwiki.com/wiki/Soulcalibur_VI#Disable_post-processing_effects
title: SOULCALIBUR VI
